trial_identity.brief_title: Phase 2 Placebo-Controlled Study to Assess the Safety and Efficacy of ESK-001 in Active Systemic Lupus Erythematosus
trial_identity.official_title: A Phase 2, Multicenter, Multinational, Randomized, Double-blind, Placebo-Controlled Study to Assess the Safety, Efficacy, and Pharmacokinetics of Multiple Dose Levels of ESK-001 in Adult Patients With Systemic Lupus Erythematosus

missing_data_flags.reason: RxNorm returned no match.

primary_endpoint_measures: To compare the effect on disease activity measured by the proportion of patients achieving British Isles Lupus Assessment Group (BILAG)-based Composite Lupus Assessment (BICLA) response at Week 48 between doses of ESK-001 and placebo
secondary_endpoint_measures: To assess the safety and tolerability of multiple dose levels of ESK-001
To compare the effect on disease activity measured by the proportion of patients achieving an SLE Responder Index of ≥4 (SRI[4]) response at Week 48
To compare corticosteroid use in patients at Week 48

To compare the effect on cutaneous disease activity measured by the proportion of patients with a CLASI activity score of ≥8 at baseline achieving ≥ 50% reduction in the CLASI activity score at Week 48 between doses of ESK-001 and placebo
To compare the effect on disease activity measured by the proportion of patients achieving BICLA responses at Week 48 between doses of ESK-001 and placebo by stratification groups
To compare the Lupus Low Disease Activity State (LLDAS) response between doses of ESK-001 and placebo at Week 48
To compare the annualized flare rate through Week 48
To use the SF-36 (Short Form-36 item QoL measure) to compare the effect on health-related quality of life (HRQOL) between doses of ESK-001 and placebo
To use the SLE-specific Lupus Quality of Life (LQoL) questionnaire to compare the effect on health-related quality of life (HRQOL) between doses of ESK-001 and placebo
To compare disease-specific QoL between doses of ESK-001 and placebo
To compare disease-specific QoL between doses of ESK-001 and placebo
To compare disease-specific QoL between doses of ESK-001 and placebo
To compare Fatigue measured by FACIT-F between doses of ESK-001 and placebo
To compare patient global assessment of disease activity (PtGA) between doses of ESK-001 and placebo
trial_design_features.eligibility_summary: Inclusion Criteria: Patients with 6 or more months of SLE according to the 2019 EULAR/ACR criteria, have positive autoantibodies or low complement at screening, and have active SLE as measured by SLEDAI-2K of 6 or more, or 4 or more if joint involvement is present. Patients need to be on treatment which can be: * A stable dose of oral corticosteroid (≤40 mg/day prednisone or equivalent) for a minimum of 2 weeks prior to signing of the informed consent form (ICF) at the Screening Visit. The dose

risk_factors: Primary endpoint is a composite responder outcome (BICLA at Week 48), which can be sensitive to component definitions, adjudication, and missing-data handling; registry does not provide estimand/responder algorithm details.

Endpoint hierarchy/handling of multiplicity across multiple dose levels and many secondary endpoints is not described in the provided record, creating interpretation risk if primary is not met or if multiple comparisons are emphasized.

Some secondary endpoint labels appear internally inconsistent (e.g., several listed as “disease-specific QoL” but are actually disease activity instruments such as SLEDAI-2K/BILAG/PGA), creating ambiguity about endpoint intent and analysis grouping.

No posted results and no public product label/safety context available in supplied sources, increasing credibility/interpretability risk for endpoint-readout expectations (without inferring outcomes).
endpoint_risk_assessment.rationale: NCT05966480 is a Phase 2, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled SLE study with a single primary clinical composite responder endpoint (BICLA response at Week 48). This is a clinically meaningful (non-surrogate) endpoint family but is composite and operationally complex; the supplied registry excerpt does not specify key analysis details (responder definition implementation, estimand, missing data strategy, multiplicity across dose arms/secondary endpoints), and some secondary endpoint wording is inconsistent, which elevates ambiguity risk despite the endpoint being standard in SLE trials.

missing_data_flags.reason: No details provided on estimand (treatment policy vs hypothetical), intercurrent event handling (e.g., rescue meds), or missing data/imputation rules for BICLA/SRI/PROs.
Registry excerpt does not describe multiplicity control across multiple dose levels versus placebo and numerous secondary endpoints.
No source-provided detail on exact BICLA responder algorithm/components and assessment windows as implemented for the primary endpoint.
Several secondary endpoints are labeled as QoL but reference disease activity indices (SLEDAI-2K, BILAG-2004, PGA), which may reflect registry wording issues and creates interpretation ambiguity.
No usable openFDA label match was available in supplied data; limits safety-context cross-checking for AE-focused endpoints.

enrollment_duration_risk.rationale: Registry lists ACTUAL enrollment of 408 in a Phase 2, multicenter, multinational SLE trial with broad global site footprint (169 sites across 21 countries/territories). While eligibility requires seropositivity/low complement and active SLE (SLEDAI-2K threshold) and excludes severe nephritis/neuropsychiatric SLE and significant infection history (which can add screening burden), the large enrolled sample and extensive geography support low enrollment/duration risk; trial is currently Active, Not Recruiting (suggesting enrollment is no longer the gating item).

assumptions.name: planned_duration_months
assumptions.provenance: ClinicalTrials.gov start_date and primary_completion_date month difference

comparator_benchmarking.benchmark_summary: ClinicalTrials.gov search found 10 public comparator trials for SLE and PHASE2. Most candidates were excluded due to major modality (CAR-T/device), indication focus (cutaneous lupus/surgery), or design differences (single-arm, LTE). The closest comparator is the Phase 2 randomized placebo-controlled SLE responder trial NCT01135459; several others are weak matches due to LTE design, short biomarker-focused endpoints, or cutaneous/joint-enriched populations.
comparator_benchmarking.status_summary: ACTIVE_NOT_RECRUITING: 2; COMPLETED: 2; ENROLLING_BY_INVITATION: 1; NOT_YET_RECRUITING: 2; RECRUITING: 1; UNKNOWN: 2
comparator_benchmarking.sponsor_summary: Biogen: 2; Buddhist Tzu Chi General Hospital: 1; Cephalon, Inc.: 1; Jemincare: 1; Merck Healthcare KGaA, Darmstadt, Germany, an affiliate of Merck KGaA, Darmstadt, Germany: 1; Miltenyi Biomedicine GmbH: 1; NYU Langone Health: 1; Truway Health, Inc.: 1; iCell Gene Therapeutics: 1
comparator_benchmarking.endpoint_summary: 19 primary endpoints were normalized across 10 trials.

missing_data_flags.reason: Registry extract does not state ESK-001/envudeucitinib MOA/target, limiting MOA-based matching.
Most comparator records do not specify MOA/target in supplied fields; matching is based on modality/route/design/endpoints only.
Disease area could not be mapped to workbook labels.

approval_likelihood_proxy.basis: No source-backed historical PoS was available, so no approval likelihood proxy was calculated.

likely_failure_modes.rationale: Primary endpoint is a Week-48 BICLA composite responder outcome; composite endpoints are operationally complex and sensitive to component definitions/adjudication and intercurrent events, but the provided registry extract lacks details on the exact responder algorithm/assessment windows and estimand.

Key analysis details are not provided in supplied sources (estimand, intercurrent event handling such as rescue meds/steroid changes, missing-data/imputation rules, and multiplicity control across multiple dose levels and many secondary endpoints), increasing interpretability risk.

Large, multinational footprint (169 sites across many countries) and long treatment horizon to Week 48 increase cross-site consistency, assessment standardization, and retention risk, particularly for composite disease-activity measures and multiple PRO/QoL instruments.

No usable public product label match was available in supplied sources, limiting source-backed assessment of class/known risks; protocol exclusions emphasize infection/herpes history and the trial includes extensive lab/ECG monitoring, indicating safety monitoring burden and potential infection-related risk sensitivity without quantification.

Placebo-controlled design is straightforward, but background SOC heterogeneity (allowed corticosteroids/antimalarials/one conventional DMARD) can add variability that may interact with responder composites if not tightly controlled in analysis (details not provided).

Mechanism of action/target is not stated in the supplied registry extract, limiting ability to assess biomarker-enrichment alignment (seropositivity/low complement and active SLE entry criteria) versus expected pharmacology and to benchmark against mechanism-similar trials.

Enrollment risk appears lower because ACTUAL enrollment is 408 and status is Active, Not Recruiting; however, inclusion requires seropositivity/low complement and active SLE and multiple infection exclusions may have increased screening burden earlier (no screen-failure data provided).

safety_context.summary: No openFDA product label match was available for envudeucitinib/ESK-001; therefore, no label-derived safety risks, boxed warnings, contraindications, or monitoring requirements can be asserted from provided evidence. Registry-only safety context: trial excludes participants with infection risk factors (e.g., primary immunodeficiency/HIV/splenectomy; active or clinically significant recent infections; severe herpes infection history including disseminated herpes, herpes encephalitis, recurrent zoster, ophthalmic herpes; recent active zoster or HSV), and excludes severe/unstable neuropsychiatric SLE and active proliferative lupus nephritis likely to require disallowed therapy. Trial plans safety monitoring via clinical/lab assessments including liver/kidney tests, ECGs, hematology, and physical exams; TEAEs/SAEs/grade>3/AEs of clinical interest recorded.

claims.claim_text: NCT05966480 has ClinicalTrials.gov status ACTIVE_NOT_RECRUITING and phases PHASE2.
NCT05966480 reports enrollment 408 and 1 primary endpoints.
Endpoint risk level is medium based on registry primary endpoint structure.
Enrollment and duration risk level is low.
ClinicalTrials.gov comparator benchmarking matched 10 public trials.
Failure-mode classification overall risk level is medium.
The inferred clinical asset for NCT05966480 is Envudeucitinib.
Clinical label-expansion rationale was structured from registry condition context and available public label context.
